Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-3). It is a dataset-agnostic BLUEPRINT engine for rigorous empirical ML/NLP. Hard rules: the engine imports NO dataset/adapter specifics (ADR-0002); all engine computation/storage/observability + the bulk reasoning tier run on-box (ADR-0003) while the optional high-stakes tier is off-box by design; optional heavy backends are lazy-imported behind extras (ADR-0006).

This is the THIRD hardening pass. Passes 1 and 2 already fixed findings #101-#156 — read docs/hardening/PASS-1.md and docs/hardening/PASS-2.md and DO NOT re-report anything already fixed there. Use /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python and /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/ruff if you want to run a PoC (read-only; do not commit). Be adversarial, precise, and concrete: every finding needs a real file:line, a crisp statement of the production-readiness defect, a PoC or mutation demonstrating it where possible, and a specific suggested fix. Prefer a few HIGH-confidence real defects over many speculative nits. If the area is genuinely clean, say so and return few/no findings.

YOUR DIMENSION: Observability & provenance: tracing/span/baggage correctness, experiment-tracker run lifecycle (start/end, leaks), provenance entry completeness/ordering, that the recorded trail actually lets an auditor reconstruct the run, MLflow/OTel wiring correctness vs best practice.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

90	### 4. [HIGH] HIGH_STAKES tier has no inner timeout/cancellation — hung subprocess calls permanently leak the global pool's worker slots, killing reasoning process-wide for ALL concurrent studies
91	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py` 181-217, 361-439, 442-490
92	- **Problem:** The reasoning seam routes every LLM call through a single module-global, 8-worker ThreadPoolExecutor (_pool) shared across all threads and all concurrent run_study invocations. _with_timeout enforces only a WALL-CLOCK timeout via future.result(timeout=...); it explicitly does NOT cancel the worker (the docstring at lines 226-229 acknowledges a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked in a backend call). The slot is only released by the add_done_callback (_release), i.e. when the underlying call actually returns. For the BULK tier this is survivable because _bulk_complete passes client_kwargs={'timeout': BULK_TIMEOUT} to ChatOllama, so the worker is guaranteed to return and free its slot. But the HIGH_STAKES tier (_high_stakes_complete) passes NO timeout to ClaudeAgentOptions or query() and wraps the claude subprocess in anyio.run(_drain) with no cancellation. If the claude CLI subprocess hangs (stalled subscription endpoint, network blackhole, child process wedged), the worker thread blocks forever and its pool slot is NEVER released. _inflight stays elevated permanently. Once 8 such high-stakes calls hang — entirely plausible across concurrent studies when the subscription path stalls — the global pool is permanently saturated: _submit_bounded raises RateLimitError for EVERY subsequent call (any tier, any study), and _run_with_retries exhausts the rate-limit budget and fails the caller permanently. There is no recovery short of restarting the process. PoC confirmed: (1) 8 hung high-stakes-style calls drive seam._inflight to 8 and it never returns to 0; (2) an unrelated, healthy Study B issuing a fast call gets RateLimitError instead of running ('Study B cannot reason at all despite its own backend being fine'); (3) with _inflight forced to 8, seam._run_with_retries() on a fresh BULK request fails with RateLimitError after exhausting all rate retries in ~0.4s. This is a process-wide, cross-study, unrecoverable resource-exhaustion (effectively a livelock/deadlock of the whole reasoning subsystem) triggered by the most critical/gated tier.
93	- **Suggested fix:** Give the HIGH_STAKES path a real, enforceable timeout so a hung subprocess cannot permanently hold a worker. Run query()/_drain under anyio's move_on_after/fail_after (or asyncio.wait_for) bounded by HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT inside _high_stakes_complete, and ensure the claude subprocess is actually terminated on cancellation (the worker must RETURN so _release fires and the slot frees). Additionally, consider isolating the high-stakes tier in its own bounded pool so a high-stakes stall cannot starve bulk-tier callers, and/or have _release/health logic reclaim slots whose worker has exceeded a hard ceiling. Optionally surface a metric/alert when _inflight stays pinned at _POOL_WORKERS.
94	- **Verifier 1:** TRUE production-readiness defect, independently confirmed by reading the code and by a working PoC. The reasoning seam routes every call through one module-global 8-worker ThreadPoolExecutor whose slots are only released when the worker returns. The wall-clock _with_timeout deliberately does not cancel the worker. The BULK tier is protected because it hands a real client_kwargs timeout to ChatOllama, guaranteeing the worker returns and frees its slot. The HIGH_STAKES tier passes NO enforceable timeout to ClaudeAgentOptions/query() and wraps the claude subprocess in anyio.run(_drain) with no cancellation, so a wedged subprocess (stalled subscription endpoint, network blackhole, wedged child) blocks the worker forever and permanently leaks its pool slot. Because _pool/_inflight are process-global and shared across all concurrent run_study invocations, 8 such hangs saturate the pool permane
95	- **Verifier 2:** TRUE production-readiness defect, independently confirmed by reading the code and by a working PoC. The reasoning seam routes every LLM call through one module-global 8-worker ThreadPoolExecutor shared across all threads and all concurrent run_study invocations. The wall-clock timeout in `_with_timeout` (future.result(timeout=...)) deliberately does NOT cancel the worker (per its own docstring at 226-229), and `_release` decrements `_inflight` only when the worker future completes. There is no watchdog/reclaim anywhere (grep-confirmed). The BULK tier is genuinely safe because `client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT}` bounds the call inside the worker so it always returns. The HIGH_STAKES tier is genuinely unprotected: `_high_stakes_complete` passes no timeout to ClaudeAgentOptions/query() and runs `anyio.run(_drain)` with no cancellation, and the installed SDK (0.2.102) exposes no overal

97	### 5. [HIGH] Invalid predicted_direction silently flips the statistical tail (supported↔contradicted)
98	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/methodology/hypothesis.py:73; src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py:169-189,192-208` 
99	- **Problem:** Hypothesis.predicted_direction is typed Direction = Literal['greater','less'] but is NEVER validated at runtime — not in Hypothesis.__post_init__, not in canonical_hypothesis_digest, not in _resolve_direction, and not in the confirm functions. The Literal is type-checker-only. 1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim — "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE §3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity — honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	import threading

38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping

44	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"

46	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]

50	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""

53	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
54	class ProvenanceEntry:
55	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""

57	    seq: int
58	    kind: str
59	    summary: str
60	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]

107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return tuple(
209	            {
210	                "seq": e.seq,
211	                "kind": e.kind,
212	                "summary": e.summary,
213	                "payload": dict(e.payload),
214	                "timestamp": e.timestamp,
215	                "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
216	                "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
217	            }
218	            for e in self._entries
219	        )
220	
221	
222	def verify_records(
223	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
224	    *,
225	    secret: bytes | None = None,
226	) -> None:
227	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
228	
229	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
230	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
231	    """
232	    prev = _GENESIS
233	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
234	        if e.seq != i:
235	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
236	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
237	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
238	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
239	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
240	            raise ProvenanceError(
241	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
242	            )
243	        prev = e.entry_hash
244	
245	
246	def from_records(
247	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
248	    *,
249	    secret: bytes | None = None,
250	) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
251	    """Rebuild entries from :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` output and verify the chain."""
252	    entries = tuple(
253	        ProvenanceEntry(
254	            seq=r["seq"],
255	            kind=r["kind"],
256	            summary=r["summary"],
257	            payload=r["payload"],
258	            timestamp=r["timestamp"],
259	            prev_hash=r["prev_hash"],
260	            entry_hash=r["entry_hash"],
261	        )
262	        for r in records
263	    )
264	    verify_records(entries, secret=secret)
265	    return entries

1	"""Analysis-graph assembly + run/resume — the goal-agnostic orchestration wiring.
2	
3	The concrete nodes and topology of a study's analysis graph are the study's (adapter's)
4	responsibility — they encode dataset specifics and so must not live in the engine (ADR-0002).
5	The engine provides the reusable, hardened mechanics around them:
6	
7	- compile a LangGraph ``StateGraph`` with the durable checkpointer attached (so a run can
8	  interrupt at a gate and resume later);
9	- bound non-converging gate-rejection loops with a recursion limit, so a run that never
10	  converges fails loud (``GraphRecursionError``) instead of looping unbounded;
11	- stamp ``run_id`` as the checkpointer ``thread_id`` and onto traces (cross-store
12	  correlation), inside a trace context;
13	- the gate interrupt/resume protocol with its correlation guard (see
14	  :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode`).
15	
16	LangGraph is an optional extra (``orchestration``, ADR-0006), imported lazily.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	from typing import Any, Callable
22	
23	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import bootstrap_tracing, run_trace_context
24	
25	# Upper bound on graph super-steps per run. Bounds a gate-rejection→re-analysis loop so a
26	# non-converging run terminates loudly instead of hanging.
27	RECURSION_LIMIT = 50
28	
29	
30	def _require_langgraph() -> Any:
31	    try:
32	        import langgraph  # noqa: F401
33	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
34	        raise RuntimeError(
35	            "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
36	        ) from exc
37	    return langgraph
38	
39	
40	def compile_graph(
41	    state_schema: Any,
42	    *,
43	    build: Callable[[Any], None],
44	    checkpointer: Any | None = None,
45	    requires_checkpointer: bool = False,
46	) -> Any:
47	    """Compile a study's analysis graph.
48	
49	    ``build(builder)`` is a study-supplied callback that adds the study's nodes, edges, and
50	    entry point to the LangGraph ``StateGraph`` builder; the engine owns construction and
51	    attaching the durable ``checkpointer``. Returns the compiled graph.
52	
53	    Set ``requires_checkpointer=True`` for any graph containing gate nodes: ``interrupt`` is a
54	    silent no-op without a checkpointer, so a gate graph compiled without one would never park
55	    for the operator. The engine fails loud rather than ship a silently-broken governance
56	    graph (audit #G3). The engine cannot introspect the study-supplied ``build`` callback for
57	    gate nodes, so this is the caller's responsibility: **any study wiring**
58	    :func:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode.make_gate_node` **must pass
59	    ``requires_checkpointer=True``** (see GOVERNANCE.md "Gate interrupt/resume protocol").
60	
61	    Provenance note (#111): a gate node records its decision into LangGraph ``gate_decisions``
62	    state, which is **not** the hash-chained, tamper-evident trail. To get GOVERNANCE §3
63	    integrity on the durable path, pass ``make_gate_node`` a ``recorder`` that writes each
64	    decision into a :class:`~assay_engine.provenance.ProvenanceTrail`; without it, the graph
65	    path's gate decisions live only as graph state.
66	    """
67	    if requires_checkpointer and checkpointer is None:
68	        raise RuntimeError(
69	            "compile_graph(requires_checkpointer=True): a gate-bearing graph needs a durable "
70	            "checkpointer — interrupt() cannot park the run for the operator without one"
71	        )
72	    _require_langgraph()
73	    from langgraph.graph import StateGraph
74	
75	    builder = StateGraph(state_schema)
76	    build(builder)
77	    if checkpointer is not None:
78	        return builder.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
79	    return builder.compile()
80	
81	
82	def _thread_config(run_id: str, recursion_limit: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
83	    # Resume/run are keyed ONLY by thread_id — the checkpointer loads the latest (interrupted)
84	    # state itself. We deliberately do NOT thread a checkpoint_id: passing one is the
85	    # time-travel/replay API, and Command(resume=...) WITH a checkpoint_id replays from that
86	    # historical checkpoint instead of resuming the interrupt (version-dependent; LangGraph
87	    # issue #7361) — which would re-execute already-completed gates and break governance order.
88	    return {
89	        "configurable": {"thread_id": run_id},
90	        "recursion_limit": recursion_limit,
91	    }
92	
93	
94	def run(
95	    graph: Any,
96	    state: Any,
97	    *,
98	    run_id: str,
99	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
100	    trace: bool = True,
101	) -> Any:
102	    """Invoke a compiled graph for ``run_id`` (the checkpointer thread id), within a trace
103	    context, bounded by ``recursion_limit``. The run parks at the first gate ``interrupt``;
104	    resume it with :func:`resume`.
105	
106	    Uses ``durability="sync"``: a governance run must have its parked-at-gate checkpoint written
107	    *before* the operator is notified, so a crash in the (otherwise async) write window cannot
108	    lose the interrupt. (The LangGraph default is ``"async"``.)
109	    """
110	    if trace:
111	        bootstrap_tracing()
112	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
113	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
114	        return graph.invoke(state, config=config, durability="sync")
115	
116	
117	def resume(
118	    graph: Any,
119	    *,
120	    run_id: str,
121	    gate_id: str,
122	    decision: str,
123	    rationale: str,
124	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
125	    trace: bool = True,
126	) -> Any:
127	    """Resume a run parked at a gate with the operator's decision.
128	
129	    ``gate_id`` is the gate the operator intended to decide (from the governance channel) and
130	    is REQUIRED — the gate node's correlation guard rejects it if it does not match the parked
131	    gate, so a stale/misrouted decision cannot resume the wrong gate. Must use the SAME
132	    checkpointer the run started with (``thread_id == run_id``); resume always continues the
133	    latest interrupted state for the thread (no checkpoint_id — see :func:`_thread_config`).
134	    """
135	    _require_langgraph()
136	    from langgraph.types import Command
137	
138	    if trace:
139	        bootstrap_tracing()
140	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
141	    resume_value = {"gate_id": gate_id, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
142	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
143	        return graph.invoke(Command(resume=resume_value), config=config, durability="sync")

1	"""Human-in-the-loop gate node — the interrupt/resume protocol (GOVERNANCE.md).
2	
3	:func:`make_gate_node` turns an engine :class:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gates.Gate` plus a
4	proposal builder into a LangGraph node that pauses the run at the gate (``interrupt``),
5	surfaces a proposal to the operator, and on resume validates and records the decision.
6	
7	The **gate-correlation guard** rejects a resumed decision aimed at a *different* gate than the
8	one currently parked — a stale or misrouted decision (e.g. delivered late through the
9	governance channel) must not be applied to whatever gate happens to be waiting. The guard's
10	expected gate is the gate the operator named in the resume value, compared against this node's
11	gate; auto-deriving it from the parked gate would make the guard always match and thus a no-op.
12	
13	Two further hardened behaviors, ported faithfully from the prior platform's gate node:
14	
15	- **Re-prompt on a bad resume (not raise).** ``interrupt()`` consumes and *persists* the
16	  resume value into the checkpoint *before* the node returns, so raising on a malformed or
17	  mis-correlated value would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever — the gate
18	  would be permanently bricked. Instead the node validates inside a loop and re-fires
19	  ``interrupt()`` (carrying the error) on a bad value, so the operator can re-submit and
20	  actually recover.
21	- **Terminal-decision idempotency.** An ``approved`` decision is terminal: re-entering an
22	  already-approved gate is a no-op (it does not re-prompt or re-record). ``rejected`` and
23	  ``deferred`` are deliberately NOT terminal, so a reject→revise→re-review loop re-fires the
24	  interrupt as intended.
25	
26	The node returns a partial state update appending one record to ``gate_decisions``; a study's
27	graph state should declare ``gate_decisions`` with a list/add reducer so records accumulate
28	across gates. The decision/recording validator (:func:`apply_gate_decision`) is pure and
29	tested offline; only the ``interrupt`` call requires LangGraph (the optional ``orchestration``
30	extra, imported lazily).
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
36	
37	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
38	
39	GateProposal = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], Mapping[str, Any]]
40	
41	_VALID_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved", "rejected", "deferred"})
42	
43	
44	def apply_gate_decision(gate: Gate, resumed: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
45	    """Validate a resumed operator decision and return the state update to record it.
46	
47	    Raises :class:`GateError` if the resume value is malformed, targets a different gate
48	    (correlation guard), or carries an unrecognized decision.
49	    """
50	    if not isinstance(resumed, Mapping):
51	        raise GateError(
52	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resume value must be a mapping, got {type(resumed).__name__}"
53	        )
54	    target = resumed.get("gate_id")
55	    if target != gate.name:
56	        raise GateError(
57	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resumed decision targets {target!r} — stale or misrouted; "
58	            "rejecting (gate-correlation guard)"
59	        )
60	    decision = str(resumed.get("decision", "")).strip().lower()
61	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
62	        raise GateError(
63	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: invalid decision {decision!r} "
64	            f"(expected one of {sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)})"
65	        )
66	    # A rejection/deferral with no rationale is governance-relevant; record a sentinel rather
67	    # than a blank (incl. whitespace-only) so the audit trail is never silently empty.
68	    rationale = str(resumed.get("rationale") or "").strip() or "No rationale provided."
69	    record = {"gate": gate.name, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
70	    return {"gate_decisions": [record]}
71	
72	
73	# Only an approval is terminal: a re-entered approved gate is a no-op, but a rejected/deferred
74	# gate must re-fire its interrupt so a revise→re-review loop can re-prompt the operator.
75	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved"})
76	
77	
78	def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
79	    """True if ``gate_name`` already holds a terminal (approved) decision in ``gate_decisions``."""
80	    for record in state.get("gate_decisions", []) or []:
81	        if record.get("gate") == gate_name and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS:
82	            return True
83	    return False
84	
85	
86	GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
87	
88	
89	def make_gate_node(
90	    gate: Gate,
91	    propose: GateProposal,
92	    *,
93	    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
94	) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
95	    """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
96	
97	    ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
98	    asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
99	    ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
100	
101	    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
102	    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
103	    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
104	    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
105	    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
106	    """
107	
108	    def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
109	        if already_decided(state, gate.name):
110	            return {}  # terminal decision exists — do not re-prompt or re-record
111	        try:
112	            from langgraph.types import interrupt
113	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
114	            raise RuntimeError(
115	                "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
116	            ) from exc
117	        proposal = dict(propose(state))
118	        current: dict[str, Any] = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal}
119	        # Validate inside a loop: on a bad/mis-correlated resume, re-fire interrupt (next
120	        # resume index) carrying the error rather than raising — a raise would strand the
121	        # bad value in the checkpoint and replay it forever, bricking the gate.
122	        while True:
123	            resumed = interrupt(current)
124	            try:
125	                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
126	            except GateError as exc:
127	                current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
128	                continue
129	            if recorder is not None:
130	                for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
131	                    recorder(rec)  # land the decision in the hash-chained provenance trail
132	            return update
133	
134	    return node
135

content.text: There IS an `unfreeze`/thaw function in `_frozen.py` (line 137) whose docstring explicitly says "The inverse of freeze for serialization... json.dumps does not know FrozenDict". This function EXISTS precisely to fix this, but `to_records()` in provenance.py does NOT use it — it only does a shallow `dict(e.payload)` at line 209. content.content:
133	
134	def unfreeze(value: Any) -> Any:
135	    """Recursively convert a frozen structure back into plain JSON-serializable containers.
136	
137	    The inverse of :func:`freeze` for serialization: :class:`FrozenDict` → ``dict``,
138	    ``frozenset`` → sorted ``list`` (stable order for reproducible output), ``tuple`` →
139	    ``list``. Leaves are returned unchanged. ``json.dumps`` does not know :class:`FrozenDict`
140	    (it is a Mapping, not a ``dict`` subclass), so an operator serializing a deep-frozen public
141	    payload — e.g. ``GateReview.payload`` — must thaw it first (#141).
142	    """
143	    if isinstance(value, (FrozenDict, Mapping)):
144	        return {k: unfreeze(v) for k, v in value.items()}
145	    if isinstance(value, frozenset):
146	        # sort when elements are mutually orderable; otherwise keep insertion-free stable repr
147	        try:
148	            return [unfreeze(v) for v in sorted(value)]
149	        except TypeError:
150	            return [unfreeze(v) for v in value]
151	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
152	        return [unfreeze(v) for v in value]
153	    return value
154

content.text:
Confirmed finding #3 (MEDIUM): `log_artifact` is part of the `ExperimentTracker` Protocol and implemented, and tested in isolation, but `run_study` **never calls `log_artifact`**. The reproducibility package — the provenance trail and the baseline determinism record — is never persisted to the experiment store as an artifact. An auditor querying MLflow for a run sees only ~2 params and a handful of scalar metrics; they cannot reconstruct the run (no provenance trail, no determinism record, no scorecard). The provenance trail is only returned in-memory via `StudyResult.provenance` and is never durably written anywhere by the engine. Combined with finding #1 (`to_records` not JSON-serializable), there is no working path for an auditor to durably persist and later reconstruct the trail through the engine's own surfaces.
